./.stryker-tmp/sandbox-DdfDW4/.claude/skills/gstack/CHANGELOG.md:1828:v1.3 is about the things you do every day. `/design-shotgun` now remembers which fonts, colors, and layouts you approve across sessions, so the next round of variants leans toward your actual taste instead of resetting to Inter every time. `/design-consultation` has a "would a human designer be embarrassed by this?" self-gate in Phase 5 and a "what's the one thing someone will remember?" forcing question in Phase 1, AI-slop output gets discarded before it reaches you. `/context-save` and `/context-restore` write session state to plaintext markdown in `~/.gstack/projects/$SLUG/checkpoints/`, you can read and edit and move between machines. Flip on continuous checkpoint mode (`gstack-config set checkpoint_mode continuous`) and it also drops `WIP:` commits with structured `[gstack-context]` bodies into your git log. Claude Code already manages its own session state, this is a parallel track you control, in formats you own.
./.stryker-tmp/sandbox-DdfDW4/.claude/skills/gstack/CHANGELOG.md:1843:The single most striking row: session state stops being a black box. Claude Code's built-in session management works fine on its own terms, but you can't `grep` it, you can't read it, you can't hand it to a different tool. `/context-save` writes markdown to `~/.gstack/projects/$SLUG/checkpoints/` you can open in any editor. Continuous mode (opt-in) also drops `WIP:` commits with structured `[gstack-context]` bodies into your git log, so `git log --grep "WIP:"` shows the whole thread. Either way, plain text you own, not a proprietary store.
./.stryker-tmp/sandbox-DdfDW4/.claude/skills/gstack/CHANGELOG.md:1907:- **`/checkpoint` is now `/context-save` + `/context-restore`.** Claude Code treats `/checkpoint` as a native rewind alias in current environments, which was shadowing the gstack skill. Symptom: you'd type `/checkpoint`, the agent would describe it as a "built-in you need to type directly," and nothing would get saved. The fix is a clean rename and a split into two skills. One that saves, one that restores. Your old saved files still load via `/context-restore` (storage path unchanged).
./.stryker-tmp/sandbox-DdfDW4/.claude/skills/gstack/CHANGELOG.md:1910:  - `/context-restore` loads the most recent saved context across ALL branches by default. This fixes a second bug where the old `/checkpoint resume` flow was getting cross-contaminated with list-flow filtering and silently hiding your most recent save.
./.stryker-tmp/sandbox-DdfDW4/.claude/skills/gstack/CHANGELOG.md:1915:- **Empty-set bug on macOS.** If you ran `/checkpoint resume` (now `/context-restore`) with zero saved files, `find ... | xargs ls -1t` would fall back to listing your current directory. Confusing output, no clean "no saved contexts yet" message. Replaced with `find | sort -r | head` so empty input stays empty.

./.claude/skills/gstack/CHANGELOG.md:1562:- `scripts/resolvers/preamble/generate-routing-injection.ts`: aligned with the new SKILL.md.tmpl policy ("when in doubt, invoke"), renamed stale `/checkpoint` references to `/context-save` + `/context-restore`, added 12 missing routes (full skill inventory now covered).
./.claude/skills/gstack/CHANGELOG.md:1828:v1.3 is about the things you do every day. `/design-shotgun` now remembers which fonts, colors, and layouts you approve across sessions, so the next round of variants leans toward your actual taste instead of resetting to Inter every time. `/design-consultation` has a "would a human designer be embarrassed by this?" self-gate in Phase 5 and a "what's the one thing someone will remember?" forcing question in Phase 1, AI-slop output gets discarded before it reaches you. `/context-save` and `/context-restore` write session state to plaintext markdown in `~/.gstack/projects/$SLUG/checkpoints/`, you can read and edit and move between machines. Flip on continuous checkpoint mode (`gstack-config set checkpoint_mode continuous`) and it also drops `WIP:` commits with structured `[gstack-context]` bodies into your git log. Claude Code already manages its own session state, this is a parallel track you control, in formats you own.
./.claude/skills/gstack/CHANGELOG.md:1843:The single most striking row: session state stops being a black box. Claude Code's built-in session management works fine on its own terms, but you can't `grep` it, you can't read it, you can't hand it to a different tool. `/context-save` writes markdown to `~/.gstack/projects/$SLUG/checkpoints/` you can open in any editor. Continuous mode (opt-in) also drops `WIP:` commits with structured `[gstack-context]` bodies into your git log, so `git log --grep "WIP:"` shows the whole thread. Either way, plain text you own, not a proprietary store.
./.claude/skills/gstack/CHANGELOG.md:1907:- **`/checkpoint` is now `/context-save` + `/context-restore`.** Claude Code treats `/checkpoint` as a native rewind alias in current environments, which was shadowing the gstack skill. Symptom: you'd type `/checkpoint`, the agent would describe it as a "built-in you need to type directly," and nothing would get saved. The fix is a clean rename and a split into two skills. One that saves, one that restores. Your old saved files still load via `/context-restore` (storage path unchanged).
./.claude/skills/gstack/CHANGELOG.md:1910:  - `/context-restore` loads the most recent saved context across ALL branches by default. This fixes a second bug where the old `/checkpoint resume` flow was getting cross-contaminated with list-flow filtering and silently hiding your most recent save.
./.claude/skills/gstack/CHANGELOG.md:1915:- **Empty-set bug on macOS.** If you ran `/checkpoint resume` (now `/context-restore`) with zero saved files, `find ... | xargs ls -1t` would fall back to listing your current directory. Confusing output, no clean "no saved contexts yet" message. Replaced with `find | sort -r | head` so empty input stays empty.
./.claude/skills/gstack/CHANGELOG.md:1918:- New `gstack-upgrade/migrations/v1.1.3.0.sh` removes the stale on-disk `/checkpoint` install so Claude Code's native `/rewind` alias is no longer shadowed. Ownership-guarded across three install shapes (directory symlink into gstack, directory with SKILL.md symlinked into gstack, anything else). User-owned `/checkpoint` skills preserved with a notice. Migration hardened after adversarial review: explicit `HOME` unset/empty guard, `realpath` with python3 fallback, `rm --` flag, macOS sidecar handling.
./.claude/skills/gstack/CHANGELOG.md:2550:- **`/checkpoint` skill.** Save and resume working state snapshots. Captures git state, decisions made, remaining work. Supports cross-branch listing for Conductor workspace handoff between agents.
